subject: Geography
title: Troglobites: Born for Darkness
description: Learn Troglobites: Born for Darkness in one minute.

hook.text: Troglobites are animals that have lived in caves so long they've lost their eyes entirely.

buildup.text: Without light, eyes are useless. Evolution favored other senses—touch, smell, vibration.

discovery.text: The Texas blind salamander is translucent, eyeless, and has never seen sunlight.

twist.text: Many troglobites are found in only one cave on Earth. Lose that cave, lose the species.

climax.text: Caves are islands of evolution—isolated, unique, and irreplaceable.

punchline.text: In the dark, eyes are a waste. Evolution threw them away.

quiz.question: Why have many cave animals lost their eyes?
options: Eyes are useless in total darkness, so evolution eliminated them
Predators in caves eat their eyes
